consensus_variance.narrative: Consensus (83% Buy) holds FCX with PTs only ~+4-6% above spot - the Street rates it Buy but its own targets imply roughly fully valued. Our downward variance marks copper at ~$6.19/lb as ~40-55% above a $4.00-4.50 mid-cycle anchor, FCX's ~10x EV/EBITDA as above its ~7.8x 5-yr average on peak earnings, and the historical miner de-rate at cyclical peaks; combined with the Grasberg single-asset/permitting overhang, this shifts ~2.1pp into the bear tails. Two-tailed (structural copper deficit + Grasberg recovery are genuine upside) -> Hold not Sell. Sizing = 18 x 58 x 20 / 10000 = 2.09.

revision_velocity.breadth_note: Mixed: Goldman ->$75, Barclays init $77, Bernstein Hold ->$59; one FY26 EPS cut $2.81->$2.32 on the Grasberg guide. 13 Strong Buy / 6 Buy / 3 Hold / 1 Sell; PTs ~flat at +4-6% above spot.

revision_velocity.pre_print_drift: Stock +55% over 1yr to 52-week high $72.09; next print ~2026-08-05.
revision_velocity.peer_comparison: SCCO richer (~20x) on jurisdiction/growth; RIO cheaper (~7-8x); FCX above its own history.
revision_velocity.verdict: Estimates flat-to-down on Grasberg; consensus 'Buy' belied by PTs at spot. Caution is peak-copper multiple + Grasberg, not an estimate collapse -> Hold not Sell.
